remember: Students will be able to recall the classification of amines and their general methods of preparation.
Students will be able to identify the different types of amines (primary, secondary and tertiary) and their structural representations.
Students will be able to recall the physical and chemical properties of amines.
understand: Students will be able to understand the basic chemistry behind the preparation of amines through the reaction of ammonia with alkyl halides.
Students will be able to explain the mechanism of the reaction of amines with nitrous acid to form diazonium salts.
Students will be able to understand the general reactions of amines, including their basic nature, reaction with acids and sodium nitrite.
apply: Students will be able to apply the knowledge of amine chemistry to predict the products of different reactions involving amines.
Students will be able to use the properties of amines to identify them in different mixtures.
Students will be able to design experiments to prepare different types of amines.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the advantages and limitations of different methods of preparation of amines.
Students will be able to compare the physical and chemical properties of different types of amines.
Students will be able to evaluate the importance of amines in various industrial applications.

ncert_objectives: To prepare amines by the reaction of ammonia with alkyl halides.
To understand the reaction of amines with nitrous acid to form diazonium salts.
To study the general reactions of amines including their basic nature, reaction with acids and sodium nitrite.
board_exam_objectives: To identify the types of amines and their structural representations.
To describe the physical and chemical properties of amines.
To explain the mechanism of the reaction of amines with nitrous acid to form diazonium salts.
